title: horkelia kingii
intro: Horkelia kingii, also known as King's horkelia, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the heath family (Ericaceae). It is native to the Pacific Northwest region of North America and is recognized by its pink to purple flowers and oval-shaped leaves. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. Horkelia kingii can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, which may help alleviate various health issues, including digestive problems, sore throats, and skin irritations.
